title: Failure to link nested interfaces.
body:
[test_cases.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/5557215/test_cases.zip)
Attached are the two test cases that fail to compile in verilator, both pass with ModelSim. These were derived from a more involved design that also correctly synth'd  under QuartusPro.

In the first testcase an interface that instantiates a sub interface is instantiated. A dotted reference to the sub interface is subsequently passed to a submodule port. The second testcase is similar to the first but the nested interfaces are buried under a generate for.

Hunting through the code with a debugger all the bits and pieces seem to be generated correctly. However it appears that LinkDot isn't able to resolve a link to an interface passed to a submodule unless it's in the same scope as the submodule instantiation. I attempted a patch myself but unfortunately my knowledge of the verilator dark arts was not up to the task and my 'fix' just broke a whole lot of other things.

Feel free to use the attached testcases as you wish. 
Thanks!
